title: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow Sunday
display_title: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow Sunday
wikitext:
[]: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow (Sunday)             Quote Post by AWinters » Sat Nov 13, 2021 6:03 pm Sorry for the short notice. I'm surprised I haven't seen it announced here yet and the seats don't seem to be selling as well as I thought they would (there were loads to choose from). Odeon cinemas (and possibly Cineworld) across the UK are showing Death True² and EoE tomorrow. Just finished binge watching the entire series with my daughter for the first time, so she gets the proper cinema experience for her first time seeing it. Exciting! It says UK dub, but it doesn't specify which one. I really hope it's the ADV version because switching all the voices so far into the timeline would feel odd. Sorry if it's already been posted, I had a quick glance but sometimes I miss stuff.

[]: Re: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow (Sunday)             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Nov 14, 2021 5:14 am You'll get the ADV dub, as advertised (as "classic"). The Thursday showings incorrectly got the Netflix dub, for which they have apologised (I didn't mind though!) and confirmed they have sent out corrected copies for the next showings. I was disappointed by the audience in Oxford - but I gather that the upcoming subbed screenings are selling considerably better.

[]: Re: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow (Sunday)             Quote Post by AWinters » Sun Nov 14, 2021 10:46 pm Thank you for the reassurance. It was fun seeing it in cinema, even with the awkward silence and giggling from the back during the first scene XD I wish I set up a reaction cam for her, especially for the ending!

Article: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow Sunday
[]: EoE in UK cinemas tomorrow (Sunday)             Quote Post by AWinters » Sat Nov 13, 2021 6:03 pm Sorry for the short notice. I'm surprised I haven't seen it announced here yet and the seats don't seem to be selling as well as I thought they would (there were loads to choose from). Odeon cinemas (and possibly Cineworld) across the UK are showing Death True² and EoE tomorrow. Just finished binge watching the entire series with my daughter for the first time, so she gets the proper cinema experience for her first time seeing it. Exciting! It says UK dub, but it doesn't specify which one. I really hope it's the ADV version because switching all the voices so far into the timeline would feel odd. Sorry if it's already been posted, I had a quick glance but sometimes I miss stuff.

The Thursday showings incorrectly got the Netflix dub, for which they have apologised (I didn't mind though!) and confirmed they have sent out corrected copies for the next showings. I was disappointed by the audience in Oxford - but I gather that the upcoming subbed screenings are selling considerably better.

It was fun seeing it in cinema, even with the awkward silence and giggling from the back during the first scene XD I wish I set up a reaction cam for her, especially for the ending!
